summary: Great audience recording captures a legendary Billy Cobham‑enhanced Truckin’/Drums/Space/The Other One stretch plus standout Lost Sailor → Saint of Circumstance.
review: The Wise audience tape is praised for its clear, well‑balanced capture of one of the most memorable shows from this run, highlighted by a superb Drum segment featuring guest jazz legend Billy Cobham and an inspired Truckin’ → Drums → Space → The Other One sequence.  Standout moments also include Lost Sailor → Saint of Circumstance and Jerry’s “Been All Around This World,” making the concert a must‑listen for both newcomers and seasoned Deadheads.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews overwhelmingly rate the show 4–5 stars and praise the performance and recording; one unrelated 0‑star review lowers the average slightly, yielding a solid but not perfect rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Jim Wise’s audience tape is described as 'great' and 'pleasurable'; no major clarity issues are noted, though one reviewer mentions a video edit that cut some drum detail (audio remains intact).

show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans note a focused, high‑energy performance likely because the band knew they were recording for live releases; transitions (especially into The Other One) are highlighted as exceptional.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His vocal on ‘Been All Around This World’ is singled out as amazing.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum segment with guest Billy Cobham is called one of the best ever, with three drummers “swarming over The Beast like salmon.”
